review_title: Useful for firewall log visibility and compliance reporting
description: In our environment, we used Firewall Analyzer mostly in banking and enterprise networks where visibility into firewall logs is critical. It helped us centralize logs and reduce the time spent manually checking firewall activity. Alerting works, but needs proper tuning to avoid noise. Deployment was manageable, but not exactly plug-and-play, we had to spend time configuring devices and optimizing storage. Overall, it’s a practical solution for log analysis and reporting, especially for compliance and monitoring, but it requires some effort to get the best out of it.
author: Aashutosh C.
pros: We used ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer mainly for firewall log monitoring and compliance reporting. What stood out for us is how quickly it starts giving visibility once integrated. Log collection and report generation are straightforward, and the predefined compliance reports (for auditing and internal reviews) save a lot of time. The dashboard is also quite useful for getting a quick overview of traffic, top applications, and security events without digging too deep.
cons: The UI feels a bit outdated and can be confusing initially, especially for new users. Some reports are not very customizable, so we had to export and adjust them manually. Performance can also drop when handling large volumes of logs, which is common in enterprise environments. Initial setup and tuning require effort to get meaningful alerts.

review_title: A worthy investment
description: It was not the simplest to setup, but the documentation was good and overall setup wasn't an issue. The fact is can integrate with EntraID for SSO out of the box was helpful and the features and functions you get with it through a GUI is a life saver vs needing to learn and use advanced CLI commands.
author: Rian S.
pros: The best part about Firewall Analyzer for our business is how user-intuitive it is. For new members of the IT team, who may or may not have worked with our vendor of firewall before, being able to look through the logs and events with a GUI is so much more simple than trying to look for events via the CLI.
cons: The main thing I dislike about Firewall Analyzer is that it can sometimes bug out when changing display settings on the portal, e.g. to include DNS names will sometimes cause it to crash and not remember the settings. Editing the NOC dashboard can also sometimes cause it to crash.

review_title: Manage Engine Firewall Analyzer Optimize and secure your Firewall
description: The overall experience with firewall analyzer has been good and satisfactory. It meets our requirement
author: Dhanasekar N.
pros: Manage Engine Firewall analyzer helps us in optimizing the firewall. Identifying the redundant and obsolete rule. Securing the firewall .
cons: Nothing much to dislike. They could allow option to modify the api as per our configuration. The integration of checkpoint firewall could be made a little easier.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Effective firewall monitoring and compliance reporting solution
description: Overall experience has been positive, with good firewall visibility and useful compliance reporting after initial setup.
author: Souvik C.
pros: It provides clear visibility into firewall traffic and security events, making audits, troubleshooting, and compliance reporting much easier.
cons: The initial setup and log configuration can be time-consuming, and the interface could be more modern.

review_title: I have remediated my PCI findings about firewall activity monitoring
description: Fast to implement and get results. Very powerful. Ver straightforward to accomplish PCI remediations.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Good cost/benefit ratio. I have remediated my PCI findings about firewall activity monitoring. On the other hand, now I have a fast query tool for forensics and I have found specific devices that generated useless denied traffic.
cons: UI can be improved, not very intuitive. Search features can be improved too, I needed to export an excel file to perform an advanced search.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer
description: Overall experience with ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer
author: Shaik Mohammed Aslam B.
pros: ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer provides detailed firewall reports, real-time monitoring, and easy log analysis. Its user-friendly interface and support for multiple vendors make it a flexible and efficient tool for managing firewall data.
cons: ManageEngine's Firewall Analyzer can be challenging to set up, resource-intensive, and has limited customization options. Real-time data may be slightly delayed, and it can be costly for smaller organizations.

review_title: Best Product for Reporting and Traffic Analysis Robust of Firewall
description: ManageEngine Firewall Analyze offers strong reporting and deep traffic analysis. Multi‑vendor support (Check Point, Fortinet, Cisco, SonicWall, Juniper, etc.) significantly improves firewall visibility and helps during internal and external audits
author: Ravinder S.
pros: ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer is the best multi-vendor firewall reporting, traffic analysis, and easy compliance firewall rules/ policy auditing product
cons: ManageEngine Firewall offers strong reporting and deep traffic analysis. However, I found that it struggles to efficiently process very large enterprise firewall rule sets. In cases where a firewall contains more than 5000 rules, the product is unable to generate a detailed rules-based report.

review_title: Efficient Firewall Monitoring and Compliance Made Simple
description: My overall experience with ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer has been positive. It is a reliable tool for firewall log analysis, traffic monitoring, and security reporting. The dashboards and reports provide good visibility into firewall activity, helping to identify security risks, policy issues, and compliance gaps. Although there is a learning curve at the beginning, once configured it becomes very effective for day-to-day monitoring and audit support. Overall, it helped improve firewall management, security awareness, and compliance readiness.
author: Praveen M.
pros: It provides clear dashboards and reports that help quickly identify traffic activity, security issues, and firewall rule problems without spending a lot of time checking logs manually. I also liked how it helps with compliance reporting and gives real-time alerts for suspicious activities.
cons: What I liked least about ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer is that the interface can feel a bit complex at first, especially for new users, and it takes some time to fully understand all the reports and features. Additionally, advanced customization and detailed analysis may require extra tuning, and the tool can be resource-intensive when handling large volumes of firewall logs.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: 'Pros and Cons of ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer'
description: ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer is very much helpful for our security infrastructure. It is a blend of traditional and next-gen firewall to achieve security, compliance and band with.
author: AMIT D.
pros: I liked ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer as this provides - 1. Firewall log analysis 2. Network security auditing 3. Firewall policy management 4. Configuration change tracking 5. Firewall security reports
cons: Improvement may required by ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer - Log data, especially from multiple firewalls, consumes significant disk space. Long retention periods require dedicated storage planning. AI-driven threat detection required. Designed largely for network and security engineers. May not be intuitive for IT admins or auditors without firewall knowledge.

review_title: Solid Firewall Monitoring for SMBs, But Needs Polish
description: ManageEngine firewall analyzer offers powerful multi-vendor firewall monitoring and log analysis with great built-in reports and compliance tools. however, the user interface feels outdated.
author: Kevin P.
pros: Multi-vendor log analysis from major firewall brands, pre-built compliance report, real-time alerts and bandwidth monitoring, cost-effective for small to mid-sized networks
cons: Cluttered and outdated UI not very easy for new users, Performance issue for large log volumes, slow and unresponsive support

review_title: Simplifies configuration change tracking and enhancing our oversight
author: Sascha P.
pros: The ability of the Firewall Analyzer to manage and report about the firewall configuration in our organization environment has enhanced the change management. It gives detail on who conducted changes and when to assist in holding people responsible for their work.​
cons: It does not capture series of changes in short successions therefore missing specific change history. This is somewhat limiting when the auditors are trying to perform a comprehensive review of the adopted changes in configuration.​

review_title: Improves my network traffic visibility
author: Esther A.
pros: Real-time bandwidth utilization with Monitor and significant issues with traffic flow can be seen right away with ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer . It has been essential in the enhancement of network visibility by achieving the following advantages.​
cons: The first step was the necessity to open some ports which were overlapping with others being used in the network. These conflicts also required more time and coordination with the IT department to sort out.​

review_title: Enhanced our capacity of dealing with firewall logs from various vendors
author: Abdullah A.
pros: I was able to compile logs from multiple devices from the different vendors in a single window due to the multi-vendor support in the ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer . This consolidation also eased the supervisory challenges of monitoring and evaluating especially with our widespread network infrastructures.​
cons: The auto-discovery was sometimes tricky; sometimes, some of the devices would not be picked up and had to be configured manually. This put more time into the deployment and needed to be addressed.​

review_title: Detailed traffic analysis helps identify suspicious patterns early
author: Khaled S.
pros: The most informative report was spanned by the bandwidth usage I used for managing the excess. I was able to perform the compliance checks in much less time than before, while still maintaining the required standards for security.
cons: It can take more time when dealing with large number of logs during the parsing process. At times providing the required reports in the shortest time possible may be delayed due to the high traffic volume at the time of requesting.

review_title: ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer Is Reliable and Easy to Use
description: I have an incredible experience with ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer .
author: Ahmed M.
pros: The capabilities of ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer are excellent. It has been an easy to use security solution.
cons: The features of ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer are flawless. ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer meets our needs.

review_title: Provided threat detection with zero vendor lock-in
author: Nermin M.
pros: I specifically found security alerts, which are processed in real time, and log correlation with different vendors quite useful. Firewall Analyzer helped me have a one-stop view into threats, port scans, and spike in traffic by using devices from Fortinet and Cisco and no other tools.
cons: There is one area that raise functionality for me, and is related to delay of log collection in high load environment.

review_title: Expanded our knowledge of the network traffic fluctuation
author: Ouria K.
pros: ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer has brought useful features such as comprehensive traffic reports of network usage. This information has been very useful in establishing some areas that are likely to cause congestion and or how to allocate bandwidth appropriately.​
cons: Carefully analyzing the traffic data can present challenges, as the information at times may not be detailed enough, and additional instruments have to be utilized to gain a clearer understanding of the issue.​

review_title: Assisted in minimizing guess work especially in determining policy violations
author: Mohamed M.
pros: I was captivated by the capability that Firewall Analyzer has in parsing logs from various brands of firewalls. I did not need to switch between another set of interface or tool to read logs from Check Point and Juniper; it was integrated with the same reporting portal.
cons: It is even worst when the configuration backup schedule fails to complete particularly when working with older firmware versions. While such an occurrence is not very common, it entails some manual checks that use a lot of time.

review_title: Reduced latency in packet inspection and improved firewall throughput
author: Marcos Z.
pros: I consider the suggestions related to reordering and optimization to be quite helpful as well. Firewall Analyzer also featured a rule ranking that depended on its contextual use, thus allowing for the identification of frequent hits on rules that required reordering to increase efficiency.
cons: Each and every rule had to be pulled from the device by device and if there are numerous firewalls of various makes and models then this operation equals to work of a robot.

review_title: Made my security compliance and threat detection process less complex
author: Jean Louis N.
pros: ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer has interesting features for compliance reports for companies, such as PCI DSS as well as HIPAA. I do not waste much time checking for vulnerabilities before audits as the threat correlation feature aids in identifying the source of attacks hence minimizing the time spent on investigations.
cons: Sometimes it takes a few seconds to load logs from multiple firewall vendors when the filter is in use. A smoother multi-vendor sync would make it easier to use.

review_title: Improve the structure and transparency in my log monitoring process
author: Ahmed S.
pros: I specifically liked that Firewall Analyzer had no allegiance to any vendor, since my network comprised of both. I could analyze logs from Check Point and Juniper firewalls with the same logical structure to produce security analytics and set up alerts that were actually preventing internal danger thresholds.
cons: The performance issue that I came across are that we had occasional Log Retention Loss for High Volume Firewalls.

review_title: Delivered dependable firewall log management and analysis
author: Cynthia G.
pros: The forensic log search function of ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer is good as I do not have any problem in tracing an incident to the exact time it occurred. Predefined reports for common firewall models help to cut the time spent on configurations.

review_title: Enhances firewall rule management effectively
author: Ziad M.
pros: The firewall rule analysis of Firewall Analyzer meets my expectations, which I like. It is the one which points out the existing or deleted rules which are useless in the firewall configuration. This has made our network faster and less prone to certain risks.​
cons: The part of the dashboard containing rules could be designed in stance with the current format.

review_title: Promotes Unity to reinforce the firewall with confidence
author: Adrian U.
pros: I appreciate how it helps one monitor any alteration made to firewalls without authorization. I can no longer imagine how it was before when I was not able to track configuration changes in the timeline view for faster troubleshooting.
cons: If I attempt to get results for a longer date scope or across different firewalls, for instance, the reporting module takes time to respond.

review_title: Useful for controlling and monitoring the bandwidth of our network
author: Hector C.
pros: I have found the bandwidth monitoring features of ManageEngine Firewall Analyzer to be quite helpful to me due to the following reasons. This has helped us in identifying who is using how much bandwidth and the trends involved to improve our networks.​
cons: I faced difficulties with report scheduling as the reports are not always delivered on time or at least at the set time. This unreliability sometimes means that reports must be done manually and that leads to the disturbance of work flow.​
